summary: Implement M5 ignore-aware project search and confirmed replace, structural current-file outline, find references, and exact cross-file F12 definition navigation.

decisions.decision: Read root .gitignore followed by .scadmillignore with later matching rules winning; support bounded familiar glob, directory, rooted, comment, and negation forms; skip binary files; disclose ignored counts. Park only alternate normative semantics in Q-0041.
decisions.rationale: FR-15.6 requires ignore patterns without naming their source or grammar. This conservative behavior is familiar, visible, reversible, and does not invent permanent hidden exclusions.

decisions.decision: Use the existing independently authored Lezer grammar as the single source for outline, callable/variable occurrence locations, include/use dependency traversal, exact definition selection, and reference discovery.
decisions.rationale: Parser locations distinguish code from comments and strings, reuse the existing clean-room grammar, and satisfy AC-15.f without a second parser or textual-definition guesswork.

decisions.decision: Search the full stored text snapshot overlaid by open buffers, calculate all replacement offsets before mutation, write closed files first, then edit open buffers, and compensate completed writes/edits from original sources if a later operation fails.
decisions.rationale: Unsaved work remains authoritative, replacement is deterministic, and the existing non-transactional storage port receives best-effort all-or-revert behavior without silently saving dirty editors.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-15.6 project search must find literal text across project files, respect ignore patterns, bound results, and generate deterministic replacements.
testEvidence.test: Search a synthetic project with .gitignore and .scadmillignore, case and whole-word variants, literal regex characters, deterministic replacement plans, invalid queries, and a result cap.

red.outcome: RED: the new search module did not exist; after implementation the only mismatch was a corrected test column expectation (22 rather than 24).

testEvidence.criterion: AC-15.f requires F12 on a module used in a.scad through use to open b.scad at the exact definition.
testEvidence.test: Parse exact source locations, exclude comments and strings, resolve a use edge, open and activate the unloaded definition file, then issue a line/column/range editor navigation request.

red.outcome: RED: the structural navigation module did not exist.

green.outcome: GREEN: 7 files and 43 tests passed across search UI, outline, references, F12 orchestration, exact editor selection, shortcuts, and preserved minimap behavior.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
